title: My Cousins Coming Over to Watch Eva this Weekend
display_title: My Cousins Coming Over to Watch Eva this Weekend
wikitext:
[]: My Cousin's Coming Over to Watch Eva this Weekend...             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jun 15, 2007 2:39 pm Any suggestions? I mean, anything I should do to prepare her for it? I wouldn't exactly say I'm nervous, but kinda anxious for her to see it. I really hope she "gets" it enough to like it...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jun 15, 2007 2:41 pm In terms of viewing order: 1 thru 24' (DC episodes of 21' thru 24') episodes, then EoTV (since it helps makes sense with what's going on in EoE and all ;)) then EoE. Personally, I'd skip Death(true^2) since its just a clips shows, though a damn good one at that.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Jun 15, 2007 2:43 pm you need at least a good block of 15 hours and a comfortable viewing area free of interruptions. Otherwise, don't try.

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Fri Jun 15, 2007 2:52 pm Watch the series, then watch EoE, then Re-watch EoE with the commentary. It makes EoE a lot easier to understand/ really funny at times...

[]: Re: My Cousin's Coming Over to Watch Eva this Weekend...             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Jun 15, 2007 2:54 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: My Cousin's Coming Over to Watch Eva this Weekend...Any suggestions?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Jun 15, 2007 3:33 pm Hailtheplatypus wrote: Watch the series, then watch EoE, then Re-watch EoE with the commentary. It makes EoE a lot easier to understand/ really funny at times...

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Fri Jun 15, 2007 3:38 pm BobBQ wrote: Hailtheplatypus wrote: Watch the series, then watch EoE, then Re-watch EoE with the commentary. It makes EoE a lot easier to understand/ really funny at times...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jun 15, 2007 3:38 pm Hailtheplatypus wrote: Watch the series, then watch EoE, then Re-watch EoE with the commentary. It makes EoE a lot easier to understand/ really funny at times...

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Fri Jun 15, 2007 3:41 pm NAveryW wrote: Hailtheplatypus wrote: Watch the series, then watch EoE, then Re-watch EoE with the commentary. It makes EoE a lot easier to understand/ really funny at times...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jun 15, 2007 3:55 pm I didn't really mean in terms of order, but in terms of... Should I try to prepare her? I like the Zoloft idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Fri Jun 15, 2007 4:07 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I didn't really mean in terms of order, but in terms of... Should I try to prepare her? I like the Zoloft idea.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Jun 15, 2007 4:28 pm you should set up video camera and record her watching it, so you can review the expressions later. Also, depending on how familiar she is with anime, the dub may be more accessible.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jun 15, 2007 4:32 pm slothen wrote: you should set up video camera and record her watching it, so you can review the expressions later.

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Fri Jun 15, 2007 4:56 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: slothen wrote: you should set up video camera and record her watching it, so you can review the expressions later.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jun 15, 2007 5:50 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: 1 thru 24' (DC episodes of 21' thru 24') episodes

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Jun 15, 2007 6:36 pm I didn't really think the Director's Cut changed TOO much.... then again, when I originally watched the series, I basically saw 1-24 on Adult Swim, then stopped, and watched End of Eva with a friend who was a lifelong Eva fanatic. I picked up on the rest after that.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Jun 15, 2007 7:17 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Sailor Star Dust wrote: 1 thru 24' (DC episodes of 21' thru 24') episodes

[]: Re: My Cousin's Coming Over to Watch Eva this Weekend...             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Fri Jun 15, 2007 8:19 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Any suggestions? I mean, anything I should do to prepare her for it? I wouldn't exactly say I'm nervous, but kinda anxious for her to see it. I really hope she "gets" it enough to like it...

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Fri Jun 15, 2007 9:13 pm Genuflec-*shot*

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Jun 16, 2007 7:09 am Just remember to give her a little time in-between episodes to let the last one sick in. Granted half the time what is sinking in is going to get turned upside down later on but that's part of the fun.
